title: Allocation Formulas, Overflow, and Capacity Proofs · C Contract Brief: Calloc Overflow Contract
summary: A scientific grid grows from untrusted dimensions and allocates too little after an apparently reasonable multiplication wraps. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem calloc overflow contract increment in overflow-safe grid allocator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Characterize whether the target calloc detects product overflow while retaining caller-side policy and tests.
Check header, padding, alignment, terminator, and payload additions in a safe order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Calloc Overflow Contract · Theory Calloc Overflow Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 2435. Produce bounded C evidence to prove a header-plus-payload allocation formula; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Calloc Overflow Contract contract for the supplied data.
